title: becium obovatum subsp. punctatum
intro: Becium obovatum subsp. punctatum, also known as African blue basil or Lippia, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Becium obovatum subsp. punctatum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in traditional cooking and as a flavoring agent.
